vitals.terrain: rolling farmland, hilly ridgelines, winding mountain roads, climbs to High Point State Park

course_description.overview: The Gran Route is 100 miles through the scenic Northern New Jersey Highlands region, featuring quiet country roads, rolling farmland, hilly ridgelines, and a climb to the highest point in New Jersey at High Point State Park. It spans three counties and two states with 8,000 feet of elevation gain and 4 timed segments for competitive placement.

key_segments.name: Climb to High Point State Park
key_segments.description: Signature ascent to New Jersey's highest point amid lush green mountains.

key_segments.name: Timed Segments (4 total)
key_segments.description: Competitive sections with best combined time determining winners; must maintain 10-23 mph progression speed.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Challenging with significant climbing (8,000 ft), timed racing segments, and strict 10 mph progression speed cutoffs directing slower riders to shorter routes. Shorter options: 60-mile Medio and 35-mile Piccolo.

logistics.getting_there: 30-35 minutes from NYC; start/finish at High Point Brewery, 22 Park Pl, Butler, NJ. Parking at Butler High School, Butler Public Library, Richard Butler School, and street parking. Packet pickup at Strictly Bicycles (Fort Lee, NJ) Saturday 9-11 AM and 1:30-6 PM, or High Point Brewery Saturday 1:30-6 PM and Sunday 5:30-6:45 AM.
logistics.registration_details: Online registration open; day-of available at higher fee. Part of SENA Gran Fondo National Series.
logistics.bike_logistics: No racing license required; bike shipping available via BikeFlights.
logistics.local_considerations: Support local Boy Scouts Troop #49; post-race raffle for series completers; fully stocked aid with water, sports drink, gels, bars, fruit, snacks.

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic climbs to NJ's highest point on quiet roads, just 30 minutes from NYC, with timed racing in a national series.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Road cyclists seeking challenging gran fondo with KOM-style segments, scenic terrain, and easy NYC access; all levels via three distances.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for its proximity to NYC, massive climbing, series prestige, and brewery party vibe.
pro_tips: Stage by 7:15 AM and cross official start line to avoid DQ.
Maintain 10+ mph progression speed or risk shortcut reroute.
Pick up packets early Saturday in Fort Lee to beat Sunday rush.
Park only in designated areas to avoid tickets.
